era_context: Continuation of the Middle Bronze Age II in the Levant; the Second Intermediate Period in Egypt with weak central authority. Hammurabi's Babylon is consolidating power in Mesopotamia. The Hittite Old Kingdom is active in Anatolia. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its final urban phase or early post-urban transition. The Shang Dynasty continues its rule in China.

events.title: Hittite Old Kingdom military campaigns (general period)
events.description: The Hittite Old Kingdom, under kings such as Hantili I or his successors, conducted military campaigns to expand and consolidate control over Anatolia and neighbouring regions. This period saw conflicts with rival powers and the subjugation of vassal states. The exact chronology of individual campaigns is uncertain, but this year falls within a period of active Hittite military expansion.
key_figures: Hittite Old Kingdom rulers (specific king uncertain)

sources.name: Hittite royal annals and treaties (clay tablets from Boğazköy)

events.certainty_note: Hittite chronology is debated, with the Old Kingdom traditionally dated c. 1650–1500 BCE, though some scholars place it earlier. The assignment to 1987 BCE is speculative and reflects broader chronological uncertainty.

events.title: Egyptian Second Intermediate Period (ongoing fragmentation)
events.description: Egypt remains fragmented during the Second Intermediate Period, with multiple dynasties ruling simultaneously in Upper and Lower Egypt. Central authority is weak, and foreign populations (Asiatics, Nubians) have settled in the Delta and southern regions. This year represents a point within a period of political decentralization lasting roughly from 1750 to 1550 BCE.

sources.name: Egyptian king lists (Turin Papyrus, Abydos King List)

events.certainty_note: The Second Intermediate Period is well-attested archaeologically and in later Egyptian records, but dating individual years within this period is imprecise. Radiocarbon and stratigraphic evidence place the period c. 1750–1550 BCE.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the broader chronological framework for this year remains contested, particularly regarding Hittite and Mesopotamian dating.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 1987 BCE is sparse and indirect. No contemporary records from this specific year are known. Egyptian and Mesopotamian chronologies remain debated, with competing systems differing by centuries. Hittite chronology is similarly uncertain. The assignment of events to this year is largely conventional and reflects the broader uncertainty in Bronze Age absolute chronology.

graph_edges.note: Egyptian Second Intermediate Period ends with the rise of the New Kingdom c. 1550 BCE
